mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the supply side of the economy, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the federal funds rate above its 1-year moving average signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation increase in the unemployment rate above its 1-year moving average signals a weakening labor market that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation increase in housing starts signals an expansion in the housing market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation decrease in industrial production signals a contraction in the supply side of the economy, which reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decrease in the federal funds rate below its 1-year moving average signals an easing monetary cycle that boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-standard-deviation increase in real GDP signals an expansion in the demand side of the economy, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
